steps.title: Identify the roots from the factored form
steps.content:
The equation $(x-4)(x+2)(x-1) = 0$ is already completely factored. Since a product equals zero when at least one factor equals zero, we set each factor to zero:
  • $x - 4 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 4$
  • $x + 2 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -2$
  • $x - 1 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 1$
The three solutions are $4, -2, 1$.

steps.title: Calculate the product of the solutions
steps.content:
Multiply all three solutions together:
$$4 \times (-2) \times 1 = -8$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
